data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1119 GMT - Investors continue to expect the dollar to be the best-performing currency in 2025 although they hold slightly less conviction compared with a month ago, according to Bank of America's global fund manager survey for February. Some 31% of investors expect the dollar to outperform, compared with 41% in the January survey. The survey also shows 56% of investors consider the dollar to be overvalued, down from 58% last month. In the event of a full-blown trade war, the dollar is expected to be the second best-performing asset after gold. Long dollar positions are considered the second most crowded trade after long Magnificent 7 tech stocks that include Apple and Amazon. Long positions bet on an asset price rising. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1117 GMT - Investors' fear of global recession hit a three-year low in February, according to Bank of America's February global fund manager survey. The survey finds that 52% of respondents expect a soft landing for the economy, where inflation is tamed without the economy slowing meaningfully. Investor probability of a soft landing rises for the first time in five months, the survey says. A scenario where the economy grows in an inflationary environment, also called 'no landing,' is expected by 36%, down from a 21-month high of 38%. A hard landing, where the economy slows or falls into recession as inflation drops, is expected by 6%, the survey says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

1055 GMT - The dollar could fall if upcoming data show a further fall in U.S. business sentiment, Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Baur says in a note. Many U.S. companies initially reacted positively to President Trump's November election victory but the picture changed in January, he says. Companies are unsettled by the rapid succession and partial reversal of Trump's announcements, some of which lack key details. A number of sentiment indicators for February will be released in coming days including the New York Fed's Empire Manufacturing Survey at 1330 GMT. "If there are further setbacks, the dollar could face increasing headwinds." That's because the U.S. economic growth advantage is an important factor supporting the dollar. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1052 GMT - German elections could mean more uncertainty for EU carmakers, HSBC analysts write. "We expected 2025 to be challenging for the EU auto industry, but current geopolitical developments look set to add another layer of challenge." The German elections could add to the uncertainty, especially if there is no clear winner, HSBC says. German car makers are already dealing with soft EU demand, price normalization, weak EV adoption and the need for supplier compensation. On top of this, there is the EU's 2025 carbon dioxide targets and the risk of a tariff escalation. "Policy uncertainty could well make a tough time tougher." Volkswagen is HSBC's preferred stock among the German auto markers, but it also has buy ratings on Mercedes-Benz and BMW. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

1034 GMT - The Canadian dollar's recent recovery looks premature given ongoing U.S. tariff risks, Commerzbank's Michael Pfister says in a note. Canada's trade negotiations with the U.S. will probably be rocky and there's scope for a somewhat stronger dollar in the short term, he says. "While we still see potential for lower USD/CAD levels, we think it could be several months before this happens." Meanwhile, data at 1330 GMT could show Canada's inflation accelerated slightly in January, but mainly due to base effects. The data would need to surprise market expectations significantly in either direction to change the Bank of Canada's stance. USD/CAD rises 0.1% to 1.4189, having reached a two-month low of 1.4152 Friday, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1006 GMT - German elections on Sunday and negotiations for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal could prove negative for the euro, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. Markets are pricing in a relatively benign German election outcome but this probably won't be the case, they say. "A protracted and difficult set of coalition negotiations looks likely and that should see the euro trading with a discount." In the Ukraine cease-fire negations, European countries have been side-lined and markets are too optimistic. This favors a weaker euro in the short-run. If significant U.S. tariffs materialize in coming months, the euro could reach parity versus the dollar compared to $1.0459 currently. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1006 GMT - Only European pressure can bring about peace in Ukraine, France's top diplomat says as talks begin between the U.S. and Russia, without the presence of European or Ukrainian leaders. Europe's political leaders have gathered in Paris in response to their sidelining from peace talks, and are united over the need to ensure lasting peace for Ukraine following its full-scale invasion by neighboring Russia three years ago, Jean-Noël Barrot tells radio station France Info. Any peace agreement must dissuade Russia from any new aggression against its neighbors, the foreign minister says. And it is Europeans who can bring about security guarantees for Kiev, Barrot adds. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

1001 GMT - Slowing wage growth over the coming year should keep the Bank of England secure on its rate-cutting path, economist James Smith at bank ING writes in a note. Average U.K. wages grew more rapidly again at the end of last year, reaching 5.9%, figures show Tuesday. But other signs point to a loosening in the jobs market, which should translate into a gradual deceleration in pay packets, Smith says. Accompanied by an easing in inflation, "this should give the Bank more confidence to keep cutting rates," he says. ING expects the BOE to cut borrowing costs at a rate of once per quarter this year. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0959 GMT - Stronger-than-expected U.K. jobs data could restrict the Bank of England to only one more interest-rate cut in 2025, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. U.K. average wage growth excluding bonuses in the 3-months to December increased to 5.9%, from 5.5% in the 3-months to November. The strong increase in wage growth is likely to drive up U.K. inflation, reducing the chances of BOE interest rate cuts, Wishart says. Markets price in two interest-rate cuts of 25 basis points each in 2025, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0936 GMT - Sterling's gains following Tuesday's U.K. labor market data are unlikely to last, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. The data delivered a set of marginally better-than-expected figures. However, given concerns surrounding the quality of this data, "we are disinclined to read too much into this latest round of data," they say. Wednesday's U.K. inflation data should prove more significant for markets. This could come in lower than expected and weaken sterling, they say. EUR/GBP falls to a two-week low of 0.8291 after the data, from 0.8303 before the data, according to FactSet. GBP/USD rises to 1.2610, from 1.2601 beforehand.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0928 GMT - European defense stocks continue their steep upward trend in morning trade. U.S. and Russian officials are holding talks in Saudi Arabia on improving bilateral relations and potentially halting the war in Ukraine, and key European leaders discussed security guarantees for Ukraine in the event of a ceasefire deal. Germany's Renk Group was the top European defense performer, rising 8.6% at 31.7 euros, followed by sensors provider Hensoldt, up 4% at 48.16 euros. Shares in Italy's Fincantieri advance 3.7% at 8.76 euros, while those in Britain's QinetiQ increase 2.9% at 4.03 pounds. France's Thales and Germany's Rheinmetall lag behind, rising 1.5% at 180.05 euros and 1.65% at 949 euros respectively. Shares in Italy's Leonardo go up 1.2% at 34.66 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0851 GMT - Discussions among EU leaders for a joint fund for defence spending is unlikely to offer much support to the euro and other European currencies, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. "That's because the trigger is President Trump's threat to scale back military support for NATO borders in Europe, which is hardly a net-positive development for local currencies." It is also "far fetched" to hope any EU coordination on spending will be replicated on the fiscal side to counter U.S. tariff threats, he says. The eurozone's structural unpreparedness for the economic consequences of such tariffs forms the basis of ING's negative outlook for the euro. The euro falls 0.2% to $1.0462. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Sterling holds near two-month high after UK wage data
data.description:
Sterling eased on Tuesday but stayed close to its recent two-month highs, as data showed accelerating British wage growth, reinforcing bets for a cautious rate cut path ahead for the Bank of England, despite a weak economy.

The pound was last down 0.2% against the dollar at $1.25995, but was not far from the $1.2635 level it touched on Monday, the highest since December 19.

British private-sector pay excluding bonuses - the BoE's main gauge of domestic inflation pressure - rose by 6.2% compared with the same period a year earlier, the fastest pace in a year, the Office for National Statistics said.

Average weekly earnings, excluding bonuses, rose by 5.9% in the last three months of 2024 compared with the same period a year earlier. The increase marked the third straight monthly rise in wage pressures.

The pace of pay increases, remaining far above levels consistent with the BoE's 2% inflation target, present a complication for the Bank of England, which has signalled its desire to cut interest rates.

"We think the ongoing strength in pay is likely to keep the BoE cautious, and we expect the BoE to remain on hold in March and deliver its next cut in May," said Modupe Adegbembo, economist at Jefferies.

Investors slightly pared their rate cut bets following the data release, pricing in 58 basis points of easing from the BoE this year.

BoE Governor Andrew Bailey said on Tuesday however that the latest jobs market data does not change the central bank's broad expectations for the economy.

Traders now turn their eyes to Wednesday's UK inflation data, which Jefferies' Adegbembo said would be "more instructive".

The pound has so far risen 1.6% in February against the dollar, as traders factored in easing tariff fears, which have dented the greenback across the board.

Also in investor focus on Tuesday, U.S. and Russian officials met in Saudi Arabia's capital Riyadh for talks aimed at ending the Ukraine war.

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday it was crucial for all of Europe to spend more on defence.

Sterling touched a two-week high against the euro on Tuesday, but later reversed the gains. The single currency was last up marginally on the pound with one euro at 83.06 pence.

data.title: Market Chatter: Second-Hand Home Sales in Shenzhen Rebound Following Chinese New Year Break
data.description:
Secondary home sales in Shenzhen rebounded 24% year over year five days after the Chinese New Year break ended Feb. 4, Yicai Global reported Monday, citing the city's Beike Research Institute.

Seven days following the break, the increase was 41%, the report said.

The number of pre-owned homes for sale also increased to 60,300 as of Feb. 10, up by 2,212 from a week earlier, the report said, citing the Shenzhen Real Estate Intermediary Association.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.description:
Vår Energi ASA

Sandnes, Norway, 18 February 2025: Chief Financial Officer, Carlo Santopadre in Vår Energi has bought 2 000 shares in the company at a price of NOK 32.86 per share. Following the purchase, Carlo Santopadre holds a total of 2 000 shares in Vår Energi ASA.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to MAR article 19 and Section 5-12 the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

Contact

Investor relations

Ida Marie Fjellheim, VP Investor Relations

+47 90509291

ida.fjellheim@varenergi.no

About Vår Energi

Vår Energi is a leading independent upstream oil and gas company on the Norwegian continental shelf (NCS). We are committed to deliver a better future through responsible value driven growth based on over 50 years of NCS operations, a robust and diversified asset portfolio with ongoing development projects, and a strong exploration track record. Safe and responsible operations are at the core of our strategy. Our ambition is to be the safest operator on the NCS, and to become carbon neutral in our net equity operational emissions by 2030.

Vår Energi has around 1400 employees and equity stakes in 40 producing fields. We have our headquarters outside Stavanger, Norway, with offices in Oslo, Hammerfest and Florø. To learn more, please visit varenergi.no.

data.title: Enlivex Issues Urgent Statement on Fraudulent News Dissemination
data.description:
Ness-Ziona, Israel, Feb. 18, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Enlivex Therapeutics Ltd. (Nasdaq and TASE: ENLV, the “Company”), a clinical-stage macrophage reprogramming immunotherapy company, alerts investors and the public that a completely false and misleading report regarding the Company was intentionally published this morning by the website Tipranks.com claiming that the Company has suspended its stock trading due to an internal investigation.   These reports are completely unfounded and constitute fake news. The Company continues to operate normally, and its shares continue to trade regularly on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange and NASDAQ Stock Exchange without any interruption.  

The Company unequivocally denies the claims made in this false report and urges investors to rely only on official communications from the Company, including press releases, filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, and statements from verified Company channels.

“This is a blatant attempt to manipulate public perception and mislead investors,” said Oren Hershkovitz, the Company’s CEO. “We take this matter very seriously and will pursue all available legal actions against the individuals responsible, including the publishers of the false report and the hosting service of Tipranks.com. We are continuing to execute our operating plan and are excited about the potential of AllocetraTM to offer treatment solutions to tens of millions of osteoarthritis patients, who have very poor treatment alternatives today.”

Investors and stakeholders with concerns or questions are encouraged to contact Shachar Shlosberger, the Company’s CFO.

ABOUT ENLIVEX

Enlivex is a clinical stage macrophage reprogramming immunotherapy company developing Allocetra™, a universal, off-the-shelf cell therapy designed to reprogram macrophages into their homeostatic state. Resetting non-homeostatic macrophages into their homeostatic state is critical for immune system rebalancing and resolution of life-threatening conditions. For more information, visit http://www.enlivex.com.

Safe Harbor Statement: This press release contains forward-looking statements, which may be identified by words such as “expects,” “plans,” “projects,” “will,” “may,” “anticipates,” “believes,” “should,” “would”, “could,” “intends,” “estimates,” “suggests,” “has the potential to” and other words of similar meaning, including statements regarding expected cash balances, market opportunities for the results of current clinical studies and preclinical experiments, the effectiveness of, and market opportunities for, ALLOCETRATM programs. All such forward-looking statements are made pursuant to the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Investors are cautioned that forward-looking statements involve risks and uncertainties that may affect Enlivex’s business and prospects, including the risks that Enlivex may not succeed in generating any revenues or developing any commercial products; that the products in development may fail, may not achieve the expected results or effectiveness and/or may not generate data that would support the approval or marketing of these products for the indications being studied or for other indications; that ongoing studies may not continue to show substantial or any activity; and other risks and uncertainties that may cause results to differ materially from those set forth in the forward-looking statements. The results of clinical trials in humans may produce results that differ significantly from the results of clinical and other trials in animals. The results of early-stage trials may differ significantly from the results of more developed, later-stage trials. The development of any products using the ALLOCETRATM product line could also be affected by a number of other factors, including unexpected safety, efficacy or manufacturing issues, additional time requirements for data analyses and decision making, the impact of pharmaceutical industry regulation, the impact of competitive products and pricing and the impact of patents and other proprietary rights held by competitors and other third parties. In addition to the risk factors described above, investors should consider the economic, competitive, governmental, technological and other factors discussed in Enlivex’s filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including in the Company’s most recent Annual Report on Form 20-F filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The forward-looking statements contained in this press release speak only as of the date the statements were made, and we do not undertake any obligation to update forward-looking statements, except as required under applicable law.

ENLIVEX CONTACT

Shachar Shlosberger, CFO

Enlivex Therapeutics, Ltd.

shachar@enlivexpharm.com

INVESTOR RELATIONS CONTACT

Dave Gentry, CEO

RedChip Companies Inc.

1-407-644-4256

data.title: Copper dips on firmer dollar, Ukraine uncertainty and tariffs
data.description:
Copper prices dipped on Tuesday, pressured by a stronger dollar, potential U.S. tariffs and uncertainty surrounding talks to end the war in Ukraine.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) slipped 0.1% to $9,389 a metric ton by 1100 GMT. The contract hit its highest in three months on Friday at $9,684.50.

"The dominant theme at the moment is Trump policy," said Tom Price, head of commodities strategy at Panmure Liberum.

U.S. and Russian officials met on Tuesday in Riyadh for their first talks on ending the war in Ukraine.

"War ending in Ukraine is really an energy story for Russia rather than a metal story. The metal flow out of Russia has never been fully shut down," Price said.

Peace could result in normalising energy flows from Russia, which would be bearish, but it also could means the prospect of recovery and sustained growth in aggregate demand globally, with the net result neutral to bullish, Price added.

Higher energy prices could lift other commodities since it is a key input, he said.

Weighing on the market was a stronger dollar , making dollar-priced commodities more expensive for buyers using other currencies.

Copper has also been pressured by lack of clarity on potential U.S. tariffs on metals as investors seek to calculate how much duties will inflate prices on the U.S. Comex exchange.

The premium for U.S. Comex copper (HGc4) over the LME price rose to $995 a ton on Tuesday, from $913 a day earlier.

In other metals, LME aluminium slipped 0.6% to $2,630.50 a ton, nickel shed 1% to $15,340, lead lost 0.1% to $1,990 while tin rose 0.6% to $32,890 and zinc firmed by 0.1% to $2,875.

data.title: Portugal Producer Prices Drops in December
data.description:
Producer prices in Portugal declined by 0.3% year-on-year in December 2024, slipping from a 1.6% rise in the previous month.

This marked the first drop in producer prices since April, as costs fell for consumer goods (-3% vs 0.1% in November) and intermediate goods (-0.5% vs 0.6%).

At the same time, producer inflation slowed for capital goods (1.3% vs 1.4%) and energy (4.2% vs 7%).

Excluding energy, producer prices decreased by 1.2%, reversing from a 0.5% increase in the prior month.

On a monthly basis, producer prices dropped by 1.5%, following a 0.2% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: Earnings, Bonds Nudge Wall Street Up Pre-Bell, Asia Mixed, Europe Up
data.description:
Wall Street futures pointed moderately higher pre-bell Tuesday after the three-day weekend, as traders weighed the waning earnings season and yields on key bonds.

Investors also look forward to the minutes of the latest Federal Reserve policy session, to be released on Wednesday, while the S&P Global February flash purchasing manager indices (PMIs) will post on Friday.

Retail behemoth Walmart (WMT) reports earnings on Thursday, pre-bell.

Yields on benchmark 10-year US Treasuries hewed near 4.50% in morning action, inching up from last week.

In the futures, the S&P 500 rose 0.3%, the Nasdaq inclined 0.4% and the Dow Jones was up 0.1%.

Asian exchanges traded unevenly higher overnight, while European bourses traded modestly north midday on the continent.

Constellation Brands (STZ) traded up 8.4% pre-bell after Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway disclosed it has taken a stake in the beer-brewer.

Entergy (ETR) and Vulcan Materials (VMC) plan to report earnings pre-bell, among others.

On the economic calendar, Federal Reserve Philadelphia President Patrick Harker speaks at 9:30 am ET, Governor Michelle Bowman at 10:30 am, and Governor Christopher Waller at 6 pm.

In premarket action, Bitcoin traded at $95,747, West Texas Intermediate crude oil traded higher at $71.81, and 10-year US Treasuries offered 4.51%. Spot gold traded for $2,911 an ounce.

data.title: Universal Security Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >UUU
data.description:
Universal Security Instruments Inc. (UUU) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 18, 2025.

On February 14, 2025, Universal Security Instruments, Inc. (the "Company") announced its results of operations for its fiscal quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2024. A copy of the Company's press release announcing such results dated February 14, 2025 is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.1. This Form 8-K and the attached exhibit are furnished to, but not filed with, the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") and shall not be deemed to be incorporated by reference into any of the Company's filings with the SEC under the Securities Act of 1933.

This Current Report on Form 8-K may be deemed to be solicitation material in respect of the solicitation of proxies from shareholders for the Company's Special Meeting to be held on March 6, 2025. BEFORE MAKING ANY VOTING DECISION, THE COMPANY'S SHAREHOLDERS ARE URGED TO READ THE PROXY STATEMENT (INCLUDING ANY AMENDMENTS OR SUPPLEMENTS THERETO) CAREFULLY AND IN ITS ENTIRETY BECAUSE IT CONTAINS IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT THE MATTERS TO BE CONSIDERED AT THE ANNUAL MEETING.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/102109/000110465925014475/tm256814d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/102109/000110465925014475/0001104659-25-014475-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: UFP Industries Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2024 Results
data.description:
UFP Industries, Inc., a diversified holding company with subsidiaries in retail, packaging, and construction, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024. The company, headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan, has demonstrated resilience despite facing market challenges.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, UFP Industries reported net sales of $1.46 billion, a 4% decrease from the same period in 2023. Net earnings attributable to controlling interest were $68.0 million, translating to earnings per diluted share of $1.12. For the full fiscal year 2024, net sales were $6.7 billion, down 8% from 2023, with earnings per diluted share of $6.77. The results were impacted by impairment charges and severances, as well as a gain on the sale of real estate.

Business and Operational Highlights

UFP Industries' business segments showed varied performance. The Retail Solutions segment reported flat net sales for the fourth quarter, while the Packaging segment saw a 9% decline in net sales. The Construction segment experienced a 5% decrease in net sales. New product sales accounted for 7.0% of total sales in the fourth quarter, down from 7.7% in the same period last year.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In December 2024, UFP Industries acquired C&L Wood Products, Inc., expanding its geographic footprint. The company also announced up to $1 billion in capital investments through 2028, focusing on automation, technology upgrades, and capacity expansion. Additionally, the Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend increase to $0.35 per share and authorized a share repurchase program.

Management's Perspective

Executive Chairman Matthew J. Missad acknowledged the challenging market conditions but highlighted the company's 70th consecutive profitable year. New CEO Will Schwartz emphasized the focus on growth opportunities, new product introductions, and cost management. CFO Mike Cole expanded his role to include president of corporate services.

Future Outlook

UFP Industries anticipates continued market challenges in 2025 but remains confident in its balanced business model and strategic initiatives. The company aims to achieve cost savings of $60 million by the end of 2026 and maintain its long-term goals of unit sales growth, EBITDA margins, and a conservative capital structure.

SEC Filing: UFP INDUSTRIES INC [ UFPI ] - 8-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Huntsman Corporation 8-K Filing Analysis
data.description:
Huntsman Corporation, a global manufacturer and marketer of differentiated and specialty chemicals, has released its fourth quarter 2024 financial results. The company reported a net loss attributable to Huntsman of $141 million, reflecting ongoing challenges in its core markets.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Huntsman reported:

  • Revenues of $1,452 million, a slight increase from $1,403 million in the same period of 2023.
  • A net loss attributable to Huntsman of $141 million, compared to a net loss of $71 million in the prior year period.
  • Adjusted net loss attributable to Huntsman of $43 million, compared to an adjusted net loss of $36 million in the prior year period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $71 million, up from $44 million in the prior year period.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities from continuing operations was $159 million, with free cash flow from continuing operations at $108 million, compared to $83 million in the prior year period.

Business and Operational Highlights

Huntsman's Polyurethanes segment saw an increase in revenues due to higher sales volumes, driven by improved demand and share gains in the insulation and composite wood panels markets. However, the Performance Products segment experienced a decrease in revenues due to lower sales volumes, partially offset by higher average selling prices. The Advanced Materials segment reported higher sales volumes but lower average selling prices due to an unfavorable sales mix.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Huntsman announced workforce reductions in its Polyurethanes segment and is assessing strategic options for its European maleic anhydride business. The company is also closing downstream Polyurethanes facilities to streamline operations and improve cost efficiency.

Management's Perspective

Peter R. Huntsman, Chairman, President, and CEO, commented on the results, stating, "The fourth quarter was within our expectations as trough conditions continued in our core markets. Despite quarterly volume improvement year-on-year of 5% for the Company leading to full year volume growth of 6%, we are yet to see that growth translate into needed margin improvement. As we begin 2025, construction and automotive markets, which represent approximately two-thirds of our portfolio, remain subdued."

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Huntsman expects modest growth in the automotive sector in China and relatively stable profitability in that region. However, European industry conditions remain challenging due to high energy costs, overburdening regulation, and excess capacity. The company plans to remain aggressive in cost management and focus on cash flow and balance sheet protection.

SEC Filing: Huntsman CORP [ HUN ] - 8-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Chemours Company Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
The Chemours Company, a global leader in industrial and specialty chemicals, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company operates in three main segments: Thermal & Specialized Solutions (TSS), Titanium Technologies (TT), and Advanced Performance Materials (APM).

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Chemours reported net sales of $1.4 billion, a slight decrease of 1% compared to the same period in the previous year. The company recorded a net loss attributable to Chemours of $8 million, or $0.05 per diluted share, an improvement from the net loss of $18 million, or $0.12 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $179 million, up from $176 million in the prior-year quarter.

For the full year 2024, Chemours reported net sales of $5.8 billion, a 5% decrease from $6.1 billion in 2023. The company achieved a net income attributable to Chemours of $86 million, or $0.57 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $238 million, or $1.60 per diluted share, in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $786 million, down from $1.0 billion in the previous year.

Business and Operational Highlights

In the TSS segment, fourth quarter net sales were $390 million, a 3% increase year-over-year, driven by a 23% growth in Opteon™ Refrigerants. However, the segment's adjusted EBITDA decreased by 1% to $123 million. The TT segment saw a 3% decrease in net sales to $632 million, but adjusted EBITDA increased by 20% to $77 million due to cost savings from the TT Transformation Plan. The APM segment reported a 1% decrease in net sales to $324 million, with adjusted EBITDA increasing by 20% to $48 million.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During 2024, Chemours established a new executive leadership team and announced its "Pathway to Thrive" strategy aimed at driving shareholder value. The company also announced plans for a new chlor-alkali facility at its TiO2 plant in DeLisle, Mississippi, and completed the expansion of its Opteon™ YF capacity at Corpus Christi, Texas. Additionally, Chemours fully remediated all four material weaknesses in internal control identified in the 2023 Form 10-K.

Management's Perspective

Denise Dignam, Chemours President and CEO, commented, "In the fourth quarter, we delivered a strong earnings performance, exceeding our Adjusted EBITDA expectations across all our businesses. We are well underway executing our Pathway to Thrive strategy, as evidenced by our recent strong performance, leadership announcements, and partner agreements. While 2024 was a year of transition for Chemours, we have the right pieces in place to move forward."

Future Outlook

For the full year 2025, Chemours expects adjusted EBITDA to be between $825 million and $975 million, with capital expenditures ranging from $250 million to $300 million. The company anticipates operating cash flow to improve as the year progresses, more than funding anticipated capital expenditures and ensuring dividend funding, subject to Board approval.

SEC Filing: Chemours Co [ CC ] - 8-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Allegion Reports Q4 and Full-Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Allegion plc, a leading global security products and solutions provider, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company has demonstrated solid execution, margin expansion, and balanced capital deployment, reflecting a strong performance in a challenging market environment.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Allegion reported net earnings per share (EPS) of $1.65, a 23.1% increase from $1.34 in the same period of 2023. Adjusted EPS was $1.86, up 10.7% from $1.68. Revenues for the quarter were $945.6 million, marking a 5.4% increase on a reported basis and a 3.5% increase on an organic basis. The operating margin improved to 19.5% from 17.8%, with an adjusted operating margin of 22.1%, up 10 basis points from 22.0%.

For the full year of 2024, EPS was $6.82, up 11.4% from $6.12 in 2023, while adjusted EPS was $7.53, up 8.2% from $6.96. Revenues for the year were $3,772.2 million, a 3.3% increase on a reported basis and a 2.1% increase on an organic basis. The operating margin for the year was 20.7%, compared to 19.4% in 2023, with an adjusted operating margin of 22.8%, up 70 basis points from 22.1%. Available cash flow for 2024 was $582.9 million, a 12.9% increase from the previous year.

Business and Operational Highlights

In the Americas segment, revenues increased by 6.4% (4.6% on an organic basis), driven by price realization and volume growth. The non-residential business grew mid-single digits, while the residential business saw high-single-digit growth. The International segment saw a 1.5% increase in revenues, although organic revenues declined by 0.7% due to volume declines partially offset by price realization.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Allegion repurchased approximately 0.7 million shares for $100 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 and paid quarterly dividends of $0.48 per ordinary share, totaling $41.4 million. The company ended the quarter with $503.8 million in cash and cash equivalents and total debt of $1,999.5 million.

Management's Perspective

John H. Stone, President and CEO of Allegion, commented, "Allegion delivered a record year in 2024 – a year marked by consistent, strong execution, solid margin expansion, and balanced capital deployment. These results are a testament to our team of highly engaged experts who, together with our distribution and channel partners, solve complex problems for our end-user customers and work tirelessly to make the world safer."

Future Outlook

For the full year of 2025, Allegion expects reported revenue growth of 1% to 3%, with organic revenue growth estimated to be 1.5% to 3.5%. The company projects adjusted EPS to be in the range of $7.65 to $7.85. Available cash flow is expected to be 85% to 90% of adjusted net income. The outlook assumes a full-year adjusted effective tax rate of approximately 17% to 18%.

SEC Filing: Allegion plc [ ALLE ] - 8-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1030 GMT on Tuesday:

** Sweden's Northvolt has agreed to sell a business that makes electric battery packs for heavy industry, one of its few profitable units, to its customer and partner Scania, the two companies said, as the battery maker's restructuring inches forward.

** Anglo American <AAL.L> said it will sell its nickel business to a unit of Hong Kong-listed MMG Ltd <1208.HK> for up to $500 million, as a broader restructuring aimed at refocusing its operations on copper and iron ore mining is well underway.

** U.S. fund KKR > has signed an agreement with Italy's Eni > to buy an additional 5% stake in Eni's biofuel business Enilive for 587.5 million euros ($614.17 million), bringing its total stake to 30%, the energy group said.

** UK's competition regulator said it has provisionally cleared British food maker Boparan Holdings' proposed deal to buy Dutch agricultural feed company ForFarmers' FFARM.AS Burston and Radstock feed mill sites.

** H&E Equipment Services <HEES.O> has accepted a roughly $5.3 billion takeover bid from Herc Rentals, rebuffing an earlier offer from equipment rental rival United Rentals URI.N, the Financial Times reported.

** Infrastructure investment company Infratil <IFT.NZ> said it would acquire an additional 1.58% stake in CDC Data Centres from Commonwealth Superannuation Corporation (CSC) for about A$216 million ($137 million).

data.title: Earnings Flash (TPH) Tri Pointe Homes Posts Q4 EPS $1.37, vs. FactSet Est of $1.29
Deutsche Bank Comments on U.K.'s Jobs Report
data.description:
Tuesday's United Kingdom labor market report paints a slightly better picture of the labor market than Deutsche Bank thought.

The jobless rate stayed put at 4.4%.

Employment gains remained in positive territory — despite the rise in redundancies. High-frequency data from HMRC suggests some pick up in momentum too with payrolls rising by 21,000 — its first positive print since October 2024, with the private sector adding nearly 21,000 jobs — its first gain since September 2024. Pay growth — AWE Regular Pay — edged higher to 5.9%, with AWE Private Sector Pay pushing higher to 6.2% (three-month/year-on-year), said Sanjay Raja chief U.K. economist at Deutsche Bank.

If you believe the Labour Force Survey (LFS) data, the U.K. labor market is creaking but not cracking, stated Raja. Some slack is emerging, but the bank isn't seeing a worrying spiral in unemployment — just yet.

That said, survey data remain weak, he added. Given the issues surrounding the LFS, Deutsche Bank remains vigilant to the survey data. On wages, some tempering of wage growth is likely over the coming quarters — despite the astonishingly strong rates recorded in the official AWE data. The Bank's Agents point to private sector pay settlements tracking around 3.7% this year. Other pay surveys suggest some downside.

Deutsche Bank expects pay growth to slow further as the U.K. gets past the April bump in the National Living Wage increase.

Tuesday's surprise gains in LFS employment and HMRC payrolls will give the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee more time to assess the labor market and demand side of the economy, especially following the upside to Q4 2024 gross domestic product investors saw last week, added the bank.

To be sure, to see more normalization in the labor market. The rise in employer National Insurance Contributions (NICs) will almost certainly come at a price. Survey data remains consistent with at least a few-tenths increase in the unemployment rate over the coming quarters.

data.title: Diamondback Energy to expand in Permian with $4.08 billion deal
data.description:
Oil and gas company Diamondback Energy said on Tuesday it would buy certain units of Texas-based energy producer Double Eagle in a cash-and-stock deal for about $4.08 billion.

Diamondback added that it would sell at least $1.5 billion of non-core assets to reduce pro-forma debt. The shale producer expects to reduce net debt to $10 billion and, long term, maintain leverage of $6 billion to $8 billion.

Last year, Reuters had reported that Double Eagle was exploring a sale of its Permian Basin-based producer in a deal that could be worth more than $6.5 billion, including debt.

Fort Worth-based Double Eagle owns over 95,000 net acres in the Midland Basin and represents one of the last large assets in the Permian Basin, the most extensive oil field in the United States.

data.title: DZ Bank Had ING Groep at Hold
Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Arthur J. Gallagher & Co., a global insurance brokerage and risk management services firm, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing significant financial growth, strategic acquisitions, and a robust operational performance. The report highlights the company's continued expansion and its strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing its market position and service offerings.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $11,400.6 million, reflecting a 14.8% increase from 2023 driven by acquisitions and organic growth.

Net Earnings: $1,470.4 million, a 52.2% increase from 2023, attributed to strong performance in brokerage and risk management segments.

Diluted Net Earnings Per Share: $6.50, up 47% from 2023, indicating improved profitability and operational efficiency.

Brokerage Segment Revenues: $9,933.8 million, a 15% increase from 2023, supported by acquisitions and organic growth.

Risk Management Segment Revenues Before Reimbursements: $1,450.5 million, a 13% increase from 2023, driven by new client acquisitions and increased claims management services.

Adjusted EBITDAC: $3,570.7 million, a 19% increase from 2023, highlighting improved operational efficiency and cost management.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The brokerage segment accounted for 86% of the company's revenues in 2024, with operations in retail, wholesale, and reinsurance brokerage. The risk management segment contributed 14% of revenues, focusing on contract claim settlement, claim administration, and risk management consulting.

Geographical Performance: Approximately 64% of the company's revenues were generated in the U.S., with the remaining 36% coming from international markets, primarily in Australia, Canada, New Zealand, and the U.K.

Sales Units: The company operates through a network of more than 580 sales and service offices in the U.S. and approximately 350 offices in 60 countries, providing client service capabilities in about 130 countries worldwide.

New Product Launches: The company continues to develop and manage alternative market mechanisms such as captives, rent-a-captives, and deductible plans/self-insurance, anticipating growth in these areas.

Future Outlook: The company expects its greatest revenue growth to come from niche/practice groups, middle-market accounts, cross-selling other brokerage products to existing clients, and mergers and acquisitions. Additionally, the company anticipates continued expansion of its international operations through acquisitions and organic growth.

Operational Strategy: The company focuses on delivering comprehensively structured insurance, reinsurance, and risk management solutions, superior claim outcomes, and comprehensive consulting services to clients. It emphasizes a specialized focus on niche/practice groups to facilitate highly-focused marketing efforts and the development of value-added products and services.

Human Capital: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed approximately 56,000 people, with a significant portion working in the brokerage segment. The company invests in talent development through programs like the Gallagher North American Sales Internship Program and offers competitive compensation and benefits packages.

Regulatory Environment: The company operates under various regulatory frameworks across different jurisdictions, including the SEC, NYSE, and other international regulatory bodies. It anticipates additional regulations related to new technologies and sustainability reporting standards.

Strategic Initiatives

Acquisitions: The company signed a definitive agreement to acquire AssuredPartners for $13.45 billion, aiming to expand its client capabilities across commercial property/casualty, specialty, employee benefits, and personal lines. This acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2025, subject to regulatory approval. Additionally, the company completed several acquisitions in 2023, including My Plan Manager, Cadence Insurance, Eastern Insurance, and Buck, to enhance its service offerings and geographic reach.

Capital Management: The company raised $8.5 billion through a follow-on common stock offering and $5.0 billion through a senior notes issuance to fund the AssuredPartners acquisition. It also issued 0.6 million shares of common stock as part of acquisition payments. The company maintained a strong cash position with $14,987.3 million in cash and cash equivalents at the end of 2024. It also managed its debt portfolio by issuing $5,000.0 million in senior notes in December 2024 and $1,000.0 million in February 2024 to support acquisitions and general corporate purposes.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued expansion through acquisitions and organic growth, with a focus on integrating AssuredPartners and leveraging its enhanced capabilities. It plans to maintain a strong liquidity position to support future strategic initiatives and capital investments. The company expects to complete the wind down of the U.S. Buck Pension Plan by the end of 2025, which may result in a non-cash, pre-tax loss. Additionally, the company is monitoring potential impacts from global tax changes, including the OECD's Pillar 2 framework, which could affect its effective tax rates in the future.

Challenges and Risks

Acquisition Risks: The company faces significant risks related to the acquisition of AssuredPartners, including the uncertainty of completing the transaction and realizing expected benefits. Integration challenges and performance issues with AssuredPartners could adversely affect operations. Assumptions made about the transaction may prove inaccurate, impacting future earnings and market stock price.

Economic and Geopolitical Risks: Global economic and geopolitical events, such as interest rate fluctuations, inflation, and armed conflicts, could negatively impact operations and financial conditions. Economic downturns may lead to reduced client spending on insurance and consulting services, affecting revenue.

Acquisition Strategy Risks: The company's acquisition strategy, historically a growth driver, faces challenges due to industry consolidation and competition from private equity firms, potentially limiting future growth opportunities. Larger acquisitions pose additional integration risks and may not perform as expected.

Reputation Risks: Reputation damage, whether from association with controversial clients or internal issues, could materially affect business operations. Sustainability initiatives expose the company to risks of scrutiny and accusations of 'greenwashing,' potentially harming reputation and client relationships.

Technological Risks: Technological advancements, particularly in AI and data analytics, present both opportunities and risks. Failure to effectively leverage technology could impact client relationships and growth. The company is also exposed to risks associated with AI, including data privacy and ethical concerns.

Talent Management Risks: The ability to attract and retain qualified talent, especially in senior management and specialized fields like AI, is critical. Intense competition for talent and changes in non-compete regulations could impact operations.

Business Disruption Risks: Business disruptions, whether from cybersecurity incidents, natural disasters, or geopolitical tensions, could materially affect operations and client relationships. The company's substantial operations in India are particularly vulnerable to regional instability.

Regulatory, Legal, and Accounting Risks: The company faces regulatory risks worldwide, with compliance challenges increasing due to evolving laws on data privacy, AI, and anti-corruption. Changes in tax laws, such as the OECD's Pillar 2, could adversely affect financial results. The company is subject to cybersecurity risks, with potential regulatory scrutiny and reputational harm from data breaches. The integration of acquisitions like AssuredPartners increases these risks. Legal proceedings and regulatory investigations pose financial risks, with potential for significant penalties and reputational damage. The company's micro-captive advisory services are under IRS investigation, highlighting ongoing legal challenges.

SEC Filing: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. [ AJG ] - 10-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: ADVANCED ENERGY INDUSTRIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Advanced Energy Industries Inc., a leading provider of power electronics conversion products, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's activities in the Semiconductor Equipment, Industrial and Medical, Data Center Computing, and Telecom and Networking markets.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue, net: $1,482.0 million, representing a decline of 10.5% compared to 2023, primarily due to lower demand in the Industrial and Medical and Telecom and Networking markets.
  • Gross profit: $529.3 million, a decrease of 10.6% from the prior year, largely due to the decline in revenue.
  • Operating income: $36.6 million, a significant decrease of 67.8% from 2023, impacted by higher operating expenses.
  • Net income: $54.2 million, a decrease from $128.3 million in 2023, reflecting lower revenue and increased operating expenses.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.43, down from $3.40 in 2023, due to reduced net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Advanced Energy operates in a single segment of power electronics conversion products, with revenue generated from Semiconductor Equipment, Industrial and Medical, Data Center Computing, and Telecom and Networking markets. The Semiconductor Equipment market saw a 6.6% increase in sales, while the Industrial and Medical market experienced a 33.4% decrease. Data Center Computing sales increased by 13.7%, and Telecom and Networking sales decreased by 52.5%.
  • Geographical Performance: The company manufactures its products primarily in the Philippines, Malaysia, Mexico, and China, with limited specialty manufacturing in the U.S., the United Kingdom, and Europe. In 2024, the company announced the closure of its Zhongshan, China manufacturing facility and expanded capacity in its Mexico factory, with progress on a new factory near Bangkok, Thailand, expected to be operational in 2026.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of Airity Technologies, Inc. in 2024 added high voltage power conversion technologies and products, broadening the range of targeted applications within the Semiconductor Equipment and Industrial and Medical markets.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates long-term growth in the Semiconductor Equipment market driven by increased demand for wafer capacity and advanced technology nodes. The Industrial and Medical market is expected to recover after customer inventories return to normal levels. The Data Center Computing market is expected to see strong demand due to AI investments, while the Telecom and Networking market may continue to experience weak demand and inventory rebalancing.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company focused on strategic initiatives such as the acquisition of Airity Technologies, Inc., which added high voltage power conversion technologies to its portfolio, enhancing its capabilities in the Semiconductor Equipment and Industrial and Medical markets. Additionally, the company approved manufacturing consolidation initiatives, including the closure of its Zhongshan, China facility, to optimize operations and reduce costs. The company also continued progress on a new factory near Bangkok, Thailand, expected to be operational in 2026.
  • Capital Management: The company prepaid the full $345 million outstanding principal balance under its Term Loan Facility using existing cash on hand and increased the capacity on its Revolving Facility from $200 million to $600 million. It also maintained a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share, totaling $15.4 million for the year. The company repurchased $1.8 million of its common stock and had $197.4 million remaining authorized for future share repurchases. As of December 31, 2024, the company had $722.1 million in cash and cash equivalents and $600 million available under the Revolving Facility.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to complete its restructuring actions by 2026, aiming for a more efficient and cost-effective operating structure. It anticipates continued strong demand in the Data Center Computing market driven by AI investments and expects to leverage its increased Revolving Facility capacity for strategic opportunities. The company will continue to evaluate its cash requirements and adjust expenditures as necessary to align with market conditions and projected revenue.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The industries in which the company competes are subject to unpredictable fluctuations and cycles, which can be volatile. The semiconductor industry, for example, is recovering from a cyclical downturn, and any failure in this recovery could adversely impact the company's results.
  • Operational Risks: The company must effectively manage resources and production capacity to meet rapidly changing demand. Supply chain disruptions, manufacturing interruptions, and failure to accurately forecast customer demand could lead to higher costs or excess inventory.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is exposed to risks from global trade regulations, including U.S. export restrictions to China, which could impact its ability to maintain business in China. Additionally, changes in tax laws and increased governmental scrutiny could adversely affect the company's financial condition.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company processes sensitive data and is subject to ongoing data security threats. A breach could result in significant legal and financial exposure.
  • Concentration Risks: A significant portion of revenue is concentrated among a few large customers, and the loss of any of these customers could materially impact the company's financial condition.
  • Management Strategies: The company is focusing on restructuring initiatives to optimize manufacturing and support operations, which are expected to be completed by 2026. The company is also expanding its manufacturing capacity in Mexico and progressing on a new factory in Thailand to mitigate risks associated with its manufacturing footprint.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could adversely affect its financial results. Unfavorable currency fluctuations could increase costs and require price adjustments, potentially reducing revenue. The company does not hedge long-term liabilities, which increases exposure to currency fluctuations.

SEC Filing: ADVANCED ENERGY INDUSTRIES INC [ AEIS ] - 10-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Sapiens International Q4 Non-GAAP Earnings, Revenue Increase
data.description:
Sapiens International reported Q4 non-GAAP earnings Tuesday of $0.37 per diluted share, up from $0.36 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.36.

Revenue for the quarter that ended Dec. 31 was $134.3 million, up from $130.9 million a year earlier.

Four analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $135.8 million.

Sapiens shares were up nearly 3% in recent Tuesday premarket activity.
